page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon processors running at 2.2 GHz, consisting of 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 1360 GB of system memory, providing 14.17 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the server bundles 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined total of 640 GB of VRAM. There is no local storage included with this instance type, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. The server exhibits strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests and passmark CPU extended instructions. Cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth is also strong, placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests all scoring in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and overall Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a highly specialized, high-density hardware profile where resource allocation is heavily weighted toward GPU acceleration and massive system memory rather than raw single-core CPU speed. The primary tradeoff is the weak single-threaded performance and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external storage solutions. This server is designed for workloads that can leverage massive parallel GPU compute and large memory footprints, such as large-scale deep learning training, complex data science jobs, and highly parallelized scientific simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1360 GB of system memory, and 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server does not include local storage. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth, both ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation optimized for parallel processing rather than single-threaded execution. This server is suited for workloads requiring massive GPU acceleration and large memory capacities, such as deep learning model training, parallelized data science pipelines, and scientific computing.
